questions.question: Explain the algorithm for reversing a linked list without creating new nodes.
expectedPoints: Use three pointers: previous, current, and next
Iterate through the list reversing pointers
Update pointers appropriately in each iteration
Return the new head (which was the tail)

questions.question: Compare and contrast the implementation of stacks using arrays versus linked lists, discussing time complexities and memory usage.
expectedPoints: Array implementation: fixed size, O(1) push/pop but may overflow
Linked list implementation: dynamic size, always O(1) push/pop at head
Memory overhead: arrays have no overhead, linked lists need pointer storage
Memory utilization: arrays may waste space, linked lists use only needed memory
Cache performance: arrays have better locality, linked lists have poor locality
